title: Medusa Sends Something
tagline: Medusa heard about the Titan's underground river. She sent something. It arrived in a flat wooden case. Perseus carried it the whole way.
hook_line: Medusa doesn't leave the eastern valley. She sent something anyway. Perseus arrived at Olympus carrying a flat wooden case, carefully, the way you carry something that shouldn't be tilted. Inside it was a sculpture — the eastern valley as seen from the dry channel, the underground river marked in lapis stone running beneath the surface. She had made it for the Titan. She had never met the Titan. She had heard about the river.

scenes.title: Perseus at the Gate
scenes.narration: Perseus arrived at the Olympus gate carrying a flat wooden case with both hands. Achilles was at the coordination post and saw him from across the courtyard. 'Need help?' 'No,' Perseus said. 'It's not heavy. It just needs to be level.' He carried it level all the way to the main hall. Athena saw him come in. She saw the case. She knew what it was before Perseus said anything — she recognized the wood. It was the same cedar Medusa used for her larger work. 'She sent something,' Athena said. 'Yes.' 'For the Titan.' 'Yes,' Perseus said. 'She heard about the river.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Perseus at the Olympus gate, carrying the flat cedar case level with both hands — the care in how he's carrying it: not heavy, just needs to stay level — Achilles at the coordination post watching, offering help / Perseus: 'it just needs to be level' — Perseus carrying it through the courtyard, level, the whole walk — Athena recognizing the cedar wood, knowing immediately what it is — 'she sent something' / 'yes' / 'for the Titan' / 'she heard about the river.' — the case in the main hall, still level, still unopened — morning light, #FFD700 and cedar-warm tones, the care Perseus brings to carrying it visible in his posture

scenes.title: Opening the Case
scenes.narration: They opened it with the team gathered. The sculpture was set into fitted cedar panels — it didn't move when the case was opened, which was how Medusa had packed it. The piece showed the eastern valley from the north, looking south toward the Olympus Sea — the perspective you'd have standing at the top of the dry channel. Every rock placement was accurate. The valley's slope was exact. And running underneath the valley surface, inlaid in narrow channels of lapis stone that caught the light: the river. The underground river. Running south, turning east near the sea, exactly where Poseidon had found it. Poseidon made a sound that wasn't quite a word.
scenes.visual_prompt: The case being opened with the full team watching — the sculpture revealed in its fitted cedar packing, stable, not shifted — the eastern valley from the north looking south: the terrain exact, every rock accurate — and the lapis stone river running underneath the surface: narrow channels of deep blue stone visible through the carved earth layer, running south then turning east — Poseidon making a sound at the sight of the underground river rendered in stone, exactly where he put his hands on the ground and felt it — Artemis leaning in to look at the precision of the terrain — Hercules's expression of simple wonder — Zeus's crown listing left, then just staying there — #FFD700 and lapis blue, the sculpture the dominant visual

scenes.title: What Medusa Knew
scenes.narration: Athena looked at the piece for a long time. 'She has never been to the dry channel,' Athena said. 'No,' Perseus said. 'She worked from my description.' 'Your description of the channel?' 'Yes. And from what I told her about where Poseidon knelt. And where the turn is.' Pause. 'How long did you describe the channel to her?' Perseus thought about it. 'I went there three times,' he said. 'I described it each time. She asked me what the ground felt like.' Athena looked at the sculpture. The ground in it felt like ground. Not stone ground, not art ground. Ground that had been described by someone who had stood on it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena examining the sculpture closely — 'she has never been to the dry channel' / 'no, she worked from my description' — the revelation: Perseus described the channel three times to Medusa — 'she asked me what the ground felt like' — and the sculpture shows exactly that: not art-ground but actual ground, described from experience — Athena understanding what Medusa and Perseus have been doing: the description visits to the east, the three visits at sunset, the terrain knowledge — the sculpture as evidence of how well Perseus describes and how well Medusa listens — Archie watching Perseus while everyone else looks at the sculpture — #FFD700 and the sculpture as the center of attention, Perseus the emotional center

scenes.title: The Note Inside the Case
scenes.narration: There was a note in the lid of the case. Perseus had known it was there but had not read it — it was addressed to whoever would be sending the piece to the Titan, not to him. Poseidon took it, because he was the one who would be sending it. He read it aloud. Medusa had written: 'I heard there is a river that has been underground for a long time. I thought it might like to see itself from above. The lapis is from the eastern quarry — the same stone the river runs past underground. If it is too heavy to send, you can keep it. I made a second one for the valley.' Poseidon held the note for a moment. He put it in his log. It did not have a sardine on it because it was someone else's note. He looked at the sculpture.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon reading Medusa's note aloud — the note: 'I thought it might like to see itself from above. The lapis is from the eastern quarry — the same stone the river runs past underground. If it is too heavy to send, you can keep it. I made a second one for the valley.' — the team absorbing this: she made two — one for the Titan, one for the valley itself — the lapis stone specified as the same stone the river runs past underground, connecting the sculpture to its subject materially — Poseidon putting the note in his log (no sardine, someone else's note) — looking at the sculpture — Zeus's crown listed left and has been there since the sculpture was opened — #FFD700 and the warmth of Medusa's note, Poseidon holding it carefully

scenes.title: How to Send It
scenes.narration: The question was how to send a sculpture to a Titan via dolphin. Poseidon worked out that the dolphins couldn't carry it — it was flat but not small enough. Hercules proposed a raft. 'A raft with bumpers,' he said. 'Dolphins can push it.' He drew a diagram. It was a very good diagram. Artemis looked at it and said: 'The sculpture needs to be secured or it will tip.' She pulled out her notebook and added a restraint system to Hercules's diagram without being asked, in the margins, in very small precise marks. Hercules looked at her additions. 'That's better,' he said simply. Artemis said: 'Yes.' They began building the raft.
scenes.visual_prompt: The logistics problem: how to send a sculpture to a Titan by dolphin — Poseidon's conclusion: dolphins can't carry it — Hercules's raft proposal with a diagram, which is a good diagram — Artemis picking up the diagram and adding a restraint system in the margins without ceremony, precise marks, small notation — Hercules: 'that's better' / Artemis: 'yes.' — the collaboration visible: Hercules's practical structure, Artemis's precision additions — the team beginning to assemble the raft materials — Archie watching all of this with his usual patience — #4DB6E8 and workshop warm tones, Hercules's diagram with Artemis's additions the key visual

scenes.title: The Raft and the Dolphins
scenes.narration: They sent the sculpture on the raft the next morning. The dolphins had been briefed by Poseidon's detailed instructions, which took a while because dolphin briefing is not always a fast process and there were many details. The raft reached the Titan. The Titan was visible in the Olympus Sea — the team watched from the shore. The Titan looked at the raft for a while. Then it lowered one hand. Very carefully. It lifted the sculpture off the raft — it was small in the Titan's hand, the way a scroll would be small in someone else's hand. It held it. The dolphins came back. They had a message. Poseidon read it: 'I CAN SEE WHERE THE RIVER WENT.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The raft launch in the morning — the dolphin briefing (which takes a while) — the raft making its way to the Titan in the Olympus Sea — the team watching from shore — the Titan visible, enormous, looking at the small raft arriving — the Titan lowering one hand carefully, lifting the sculpture off the raft — the sculpture small in the Titan's hand, held carefully — the dolphins returning with the message — Poseidon reading: 'I CAN SEE WHERE THE RIVER WENT.' — the team on the shore looking at the Titan holding the sculpture, the Titan looking at the underground river rendered in lapis stone — #4DB6E8 and morning sea light, the Titan the scale reference, the sculpture tiny in its hand

scenes.title: Perseus Writes
scenes.narration: That evening, Perseus wrote a letter. He had not written many letters. He wasn't sure if it was a letter or a description. He wrote what he had seen: the sculpture being carried, the Titan's hand, the message. He described it carefully. He mentioned the lapis stone and how it caught the light when the Titan held it up. He wrote the last sentence and then sat looking at it for a while. Then he folded the letter, put it in a case — a small one, cedar — and sent it east. Archie watched him write. Archie watched him close the case. Archie did not slow-blink. He just watched, with his golden eyes, while Perseus sent a letter to someone who would read it and know exactly what the light looked like.
scenes.visual_prompt: Perseus writing at a desk that evening — the letter not quite a letter, more a description — the specific detail: 'the lapis caught the light when the Titan held it up' — Perseus folding it, putting it in a small cedar case, sending it east — the matching materials: cedar case going east, cedar case that arrived from the east — Archie watching Perseus write, watching him close the case, watching him send it — Archie not slow-blinking: this is different from the blink moments, this is simple witness — the evening warm, the lamp lit, Perseus's handwriting on the page — and the understanding: Medusa will read this and know what the light looked like — #3D2B00 and warm lamp amber, the cedar case the connecting visual between the scenes

viral_hook: Medusa heard about the underground river and made a sculpture of the valley for the Titan — with the river inlaid in lapis stone beneath the surface. She made a second one for the valley. The Titan held it in one hand (it was the size of a scroll in their hand) and sent back three words: 'I CAN SEE WHERE THE RIVER WENT.'
highlight_note: The Titan holds the sculpture — something the size of a scroll in their hand — and sends back: 'I CAN SEE WHERE THE RIVER WENT.' The lapis stone is from the eastern quarry, the same stone the underground river runs past.
short_title: Medusa Made a Sculpture for the Titan and the Titan Could See Where the River Went
rewatch_bait: Scene 3 — Perseus describing the channel three times to Medusa, answering her question about what the ground felt like. Look at EP029 scene 4: Poseidon kneeling, hands on the ground, feeling the river. Perseus watched. He knew what to describe.
youtube_title: Medusa heard about the underground river. She made a sculpture for the Titan — with the river in lapis stone underneath. | Little Olympus S2 EP10
lesson: The best gifts are made by people who listened carefully to someone else's description. Medusa has never seen the dry channel or the Titan. She made something accurate and meaningful because she asked the right questions and Perseus answered them carefully.
arc_notes: Perseus/Medusa arc major development: the three description visits pay off. Medusa made two sculptures — one for the Titan, one for the valley. Perseus sends a letter east after the delivery — the relationship confirmed as genuine and two-directional. Artemis/Hercules dynamic: she adds to his diagram without ceremony, it works, no drama. Zeus crown: listed left from when the sculpture was revealed, stays there through most of the episode — longest listing of the season. Titan reply: 'I CAN SEE WHERE THE RIVER WENT.' — the Titan relating to being able to see underground, which is what it does.
